Football.title: Paris Saint-Germain vs Chelsea LIVE commentary: Kick-off time, team news and how to follow Champions League round of 16 tie
Football.content:
Paris Saint-Germain vs Chelsea LIVE commentary: Kick-off time, team news and how to follow Champions League round of 16 tie

Chelsea travel to holders Paris Saint-Germain tonight in a blockbuster Champions League round of 16 clash, live on talkSPORT.

In a rematch of last summer's Club World Cup final, the European champions have a chance to gain revenge on the Blues in their knockout phase tie.

3 Chelsea sealed their spot in the Champions League knockout phase with a 3-2 victory over Napoli in their final league phase match Credit: AFP

Despite dominating in last year's competition, PSG had to come through a play-off round to reach this stage.



After finishing 11th in the league phase, the French champions overcame league rivals Monaco with a thrilling 5-4 aggregate victory.

Things has been much more plain sailing for Chelsea, who reached the last 16 after coming sixth in the first round standings.

Liam Rosenior's side won five out of eight league phase games, including beating Barcelona 3-0 back in November.

But PSG will be desperate to stop them in their tracks, especially after the Blues ran out 3-0 winners in the Club World Cup Final in June.



PSG vs Chelsea: Date and how to follow

This Champions League round of 16 first leg will take place on Wednesday, March 11.

Kick-off at the Parc des Princes is set for 8pm.

talkSPORT 2 will have live commentary of the match from Joe Shennan and former Chelsea striker Tony Cascarino.



3 PSG became Champions League winners for the first time by winning last year's competition Credit: AFP

Tune in to talkSPORT through the website via the live stream or find us on DAB digital radio through your smart speaker and on 1089 or 1053 AM.



Download and listen to all our live commentaries via the talkSPORT app.

Visit talkSPORT BET for all the latest odds and bet boosts for every big event.

Subscribe to our talkSPORT YouTube channel for news, views and analysis plus our regular goals show and for more, 

The match will also be broadcast live on TNT Sports 2, with coverage starting at 7pm UK time.

PSG vs Chelsea: Team news

Ahead of the PSG clash, Rosenior chose to rest a number of key players for Chelsea's last match, a 4-2 FA Cup win away to Wrexham.



Reece James, Cole Palmer, Enzo Fernandez and Moises Caicedo all sat out the tie, and should therefore be fresh for the European tie.

In terms of injuries, starlet Estevao is unlikely to return in time from a hamstring injury which has caused him to miss three games.

It is a similar story for winger Jamie Gittens, who returned to first team training this week after a hamstring injury, though it is unclear if he will be fit for the PSG clash.

Levi Colwill was also back in training on Tuesday, but is not expected to return from his ACL injury until the end of the season at earliest.

As for PSG, Joao Neves is ready to return from an ankle injury, although Fabian Ruiz remains sidelined with a knee problem and youngster Quentin Ndjantou is also out.



In a boost for the French giants, talisman Ousmane Dembele is expected to start in attack after recovering from a calf injury.

Chelsea Champions League league phase results January 28 - Napoli 2-3 Chelsea January 21 - Chelsea 1-0 Pafos December 9 - Atalanta 2-1 Chelsea November 25 - Chelsea 3-0 Barcelona November 5 - Qarabag 2-2 Chelsea October 22 - Chelsea 5-1 Ajax September 30 - Chelsea 1-0 Benfica September 17 - Bayern Munich 3-1 Chelsea

3 Liam Rosenior recently claimed Joao Pedro belongs in the same 'category' as Erling Haaland, Harry Kane and Kylian Mbappe Credit: Getty

PSG vs Chelsea: What has been said?

Rosenior hailed the form of Joao Pedro after the Brazilian notched his 11th goal in his last 12 games against Wrexham on Saturday.

Having bagged a hat-trick against Aston Villa in the previous game, the Blues boss tipped Pedro to play a key role against PSG.

He told talkSPORT: "Joao is on fire, he’s on fire.

"I think everybody knew when he picked up the ball and he was driving at their defence that something good was about to happen.



"He’s in that place… I wanted to manage his minutes because he’s going to be massive for us in Paris on Wednesday."

Rosenior then went on to label the forward world class, adding: "I think Joao’s in that category now.

"Consistently, during the two months that I’ve been here, he’s shown that level.

"He’s a world-class striker. I wouldn’t swap Joao for anyone in this moment to be honest, because he’s showing all of the things and all the attributes I want to see in my No. 9."

Football.title: Real Madrid vs Man City LIVE commentary: Kick-off time, team news and how to follow Champions League round of 16 tie
Football.content:
Real Madrid vs Man City LIVE commentary: Kick-off time, team news and how to follow Champions League round of 16 tie

Manchester City travel to Real Madrid in a blockbuster Champions League round of 16 tie tonight, live on talkSPORT.

The Citizens will make their second trip to the Spanish capital this season, with the two sides previously meeting in the competition's league phase.

2 Rayan Cherki got on the scoresheet in Man City's last Champions League outing against Galatasaray Credit: Getty

Pep Guardiola's side ran out 2-1 winners at the Santiago Bernabeu in December, thanks to Erling Haaland's match-winning penalty.

It proved a crucial three points for City, as they went on to finish eighth in the league phase to clinch the final automatic qualification spot for the knockout phase.

Just below them were Real, who instead went into the play-offs after ending up a point and place below in ninth.

Los Blancos eventually booked their spot in the last 16 by overcoming Benfica 3-1 on aggregate in a play-off tie filled with controversy.

With the upcoming clash the fifth straight season where City and Real will meet in the Champions League knockout phase, both sides will be desperate to earn the latest bragging rights.

Real Madrid vs Manchester City: Date and how to follow

This Champions League last 16 first leg will take place on Wednesday, March 11.

Kick-off at the Santiago Bernabeu is scheduled for 8pm.

talkSPORT will have live commentary of the match, with Jim Proudfoot and Scott Minto talking you through the action.

2 Kylian Mbappe has scored 13 goals in the Champions League so far, but won't be able to add to his tally in the round of 16 first leg Credit: Getty

Tune in to talkSPORT through the website via the live stream or find us on DAB digital radio through your smart speaker and on 1089 or 1053 AM.

Download and listen to all our live commentaries via the talkSPORT app.

Visit talkSPORT BET for all the latest odds and bet boosts for every big event.

Subscribe to our talkSPORT YouTube channel for news, views and analysis plus our regular goals show and for more, 

The match will be broadcast live on TNT Sports 1, with coverage starting at 7pm UK time.

Real Madrid vs Manchester City: Team news

Real head into the Champions League fixture in less than ideal circumstances, currently experiencing an injury crisis.

The biggest blow for the hosts is that star man Kylian Mbappe is set to miss the first leg clash, who has failed to recover from a knee issue.

Also on the 15-time European champions' lengthy list of absentees are key players Jude Bellingham and Rodrygo, with the latter suffering an ACL injury that will see him miss the World Cup.

Adding to Alvaro Arbeloa's woes, Eder Militao (hamstring), Dani Ceballos and David Alaba (both calf) are all set to miss the midweek clash too.

Meanwhile, City have been handed a major boost, with Haaland set to make a return against Real Madrid.

The Norwegian sat out his side's 3-1 FA Cup win over Newcastle after struggling with a recent knock, but has trained as normal since then.

Aside from this, long-term absentees Josko Gvardiol and Mateo Kovacic remain out, as well as young defender Max Alleyne.

Real Madrid vs Manchester City: Last five Champions League encounters December 2025, league phase - Real Madrid 1-2 Man City February 2025, knockout phase play-offs: Real Madrid 3-1 Man City - second leg (Real Madrid won 6-3 on aggregate)

Man City 2-3 Real Madrid - first leg April 2024, quarter-finals: Man City 1-1 Real Madrid - second leg (4-4 on aggregate, Real Madrid win 4-4 on penalties)

Real Madrid 3-3 Man City - first leg May 2023, semi-finals: Man City 4-0 Real Madrid - second leg (Man City win 5-1 on aggregate)

Real Madrid 1-1 Man City - first leg April/ May 2022, semi-finals: Real Madrid 3-1 Man City AET - second leg (Real Madrid win 6-5 on aggregate)

Man City 4-3 Real Madrid - first leg

Real Madrid vs Man City: What has been said?

Pep Guardiola didn't appear thrilled when he learned his Man City side would play Real in the Champions League knockout stage for a fifth straight year.

Reacting to the draw in February, the Spaniard said of the two sides' latest meeting: "Yes, it is a little bit weird.

"Not many times does it happen that the last game of the group stage - like Real Madrid v Benfica - is the first draw [in the play-off round].

"It looks different. People will agree or disagree but it is different.

"But why not? In the Premier League and cups how many times have we played against Newcastle this season? Hopefully we can play against them in the final.

"When I started it was eight, nine, 10 teams [in the Champions League]. It is a question of adapting.

"A Madrid draw is a Madrid draw. What can we do?"

Football.title: Jason Cundy has alternative theory for what Cristian Romero told Igor Tudor in viral clip
Football.content:
Jason Cundy has alternative theory for what Cristian Romero told Igor Tudor in viral clip

Cristian Romero knew what was coming after Tottenham went three-goals down inside 15 minutes on Tuesday night.

The Spurs captain was seen talking to his manager Igor Tudor seconds before goalkeeper Antonin Kinsky was hauled off in brutal fashion.

3 A viral clip showed Romero speaking to Tudor during Spurs' heavy defeat to Atletico Credit: AFP

And many believes it was Romero who told Tudor to remove the 22-year-old goalkeeper, who had just made two horrible mistakes on his Champions League debut.



Meanwhile, talkSPORT's Jamie O'Hara was convinced the Argentine defender had told the manager to take Kinsky off.

But Sports Bar co-host Jason Cundy has an alternative theory.

Cundy and O'Hara have 'gone viral' in recent days - albeit for different reasons - with their reactions to Tottenham's relegation battle.

Jamie O'Hara LOSES IT AGAIN With Tottenham & Jason Cundy!

Ex-Tottenham star O'Hara has been the unfortunate victim with Cundy playing up to his self-appointed role as king of the wind-up.

With O'Hara ranting about the Romero clip, Cundy jokingly said: "I think he went up to Tudor and said, ‘don’t listen to The Sports Bar tonight’. I think that’s what he said to him."



Tudor would have been wise to do so after Spurs fell to a crushing 5-2 defeat in Spain on Tuesday night.

The Croatian was once again torn to shreds by O'Hara during the show as he called for him to be sacked as manager.



He even went as far as labelling the Spurs boss a 'fraud' before later storming out of the studio for a second time in five days.

Tudor has lost all four matches in charge since replacing Thomas Frank in the dugout last month.



3 Fans quickly began to speculate after Kinsky was replaced by Vicario Credit: Getty

The former Juventus and Lazio boss signed a deal until the end of this season after being chosen as the man to turn Spurs' fortunes around.

However, they currently sit just one point above the Premier League relegation zone in 16th place.

Their winless league run also currently stands at 11 matches heading into Sunday's trip to Liverpool.

Tudor on Kinsky substitution

Tudor explained his decision to take off Kinsky after being asked about both the goalkeeper selection and substitution after the match.



He told reporters: "It happened very rare things in my coaching 15 years, I never do that.

"It was necessary to preserve the guy, to preserve the team. Incredible situation, nothing to comment, you will see.

3 Cundy joked that Tudor had been told not to listen to The Sports Bar Credit: Getty

"It was, before the game, the right choice to do in the moment like we are. Pressure on Vicario, another competition, Toni is a very good goalkeeper.

"So it was, for me, the right decision. After this, of course, it is easy to say that it was not the right decision.



"I explained to Toni, also speaking after, that he is the right guy and a good goalkeeper. Unfortunately, it happened in this big game, these mistakes.

"We started the game, it was too much for us. In this moment, when we are fragile, we are weak. Even the second half was 4-2, it was almost 4-2 with Richarlison. We tried to score a goal in 4-2 and then we conceded the goal."

News.title: ‘Mr Calm Competence’ Keir made the most toxic job offer ever… and detonated the biggest crisis of his whole career
News.content:
The Peter Mandelson scandal will not go away

DAMAGE IS DONE ‘Mr Calm Competence’ Keir made the most toxic job offer ever… and detonated the biggest crisis of his whole career

SIR Keir Starmer’s Mandelson fiasco nearly cost him his job.

For a few febrile days in February, Labour MPs were openly asking whether their Prime Minister could survive the fallout from appointing Peter Mandelson as Britain’s ambassador to Washington despite his links to Jeffrey Epstein.





The documents now emerging show why the row became so dangerous.

Officials had already warned there was a “reputational risk” before the appointment was signed off.

Yet it went ahead anyway.

That single decision detonated the biggest political crisis of Sir Keir’s premiership so far, sending shockwaves through Westminster and triggering a week of chaos inside Downing Street.

Senior advisers quit, Labour MPs demanded answers and Scottish Labour leader Anas Sarwar took the extraordinary step of publicly calling for the Prime Minister to resign.

The PM eventually steadied the ship, insisting he had been misled about the depth of his relationship with Epstein.

But the damage was done.

For many in Westminster, the episode raised a far more troubling question than the appointment itself: how such an obvious political risk could have been waved through at the very top of government.



Now Parliament has forced the release of tens of thousands of documents detailing how the decision was made.

The files published today are only the first tranche, with further material, including internal communications and exchanges between officials, still to come.

That means the Mandelson saga could continue to hang over Downing Street for some time yet.

For now, the papers may not contain the smoking gun some critics hoped for.

But they reinforce the criticism that nearly brought the PM down in February – that a government elected on a promise of calm competence allowed one of the most politically toxic appointments imaginable to slip through the net.

News.title: Iran threatens to blitz Brit BANKS, Microsoft & Google HQs in Dubai, Israel & Gulf with staff & customers evacuated
News.content:
WAR ON WEALTH Iran threatens to blitz Brit BANKS, Microsoft & Google HQs in Dubai, Israel & Gulf with staff & customers evacuated

IRAN has threatened to bomb banks and American companies in Dubai, Israel and across the Gulf as war in the Middle East rages for a 12th day.

Staff at Citi and Standard Chartered have been evacuated from offices in Dubai – and HSBC branches in Qatar have been shut until further notice.



According to a notice seen by The New York Times, Citi employees have been evacuated amid “heightened security concerns”.



It told staff to find “the nearest safe place away from the office” – which serves as the company’s Middle East headquarters.

Iran’s IRGC has threatened to hit “economic centres and banks” belonging to the US and Israel in the region.

It has told people that they “should not be within a one-kilometre radius of banks”.

Dubai is a huge financial hub for leading international banks, as well as law firms and asset managers.

The IRGC-linked Tasnim news agency has published a list of companies – describing them as “Iran’s new targets”, Al Jazeera reports.

“As the scope of the regional war expands to infrastructure war, the scope of Iran’s legitimate targets expands,” the agency said.

Companies reportedly include Google, Microsoft, Palantir, IBM, Nvidia and Oracle.

The listed offices and infrastructure for cloud-based services are located in multiple Israeli cities – and in some Gulf countries.



Iran’s state broadcaster said an Israeli attack overnight on a bank in Tehran was an “illegitimate and unusual act in war” – sparking threats of retaliatory strikes on US and Israeli targets.

In Dubai, Standard Chartered has started evacuating staff from offices on and told them to work from home, sources ​said.

The British bank has a large presence in the UAE.

Many staff at foreign and local businesses have been told to work from home following Iran’s attacks on the Gulf.

On Wednesday, HSBC said: “The safety of our ​colleagues and ⁠customers remain our top priority.



“We are actively following government guidelines alongside our internal plans to manage working arrangements. We are communicating safety advice with ⁠colleagues ​as appropriate.”

More to follow… For the latest news on this story keep checking back at The Sun Online

Thesun.co.uk is your go-to destination for the best celebrity news, real-life stories, jaw-dropping pictures and must-see video.

Like us on Facebook at www.facebook.com/thesun and follow us from our main Twitter account at @TheSun.

News.title: Prisoner charged with murdering Ian Huntley in jail attack appears in court after Soham killer’s head ‘split in two’
News.content:
A PRISONER has appeared in court charged with murdering Soham monster Ian Huntley in a jail attack.

Huntley, 52, was rushed to hospital last week after his head was “split in two” during the horror at HMP Frankland.





The killer – serving serving life for the murders of 10-year-old friends Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman – died on Saturday morning.

Anthony Russell appeared at Newton Aycliffe Magistrates’ Court today charged with murder.

The 43-year-old, who is also an inmate at HMP Frankland, spoke only to confirm his identity.



Russell will next appear at Newcastle Crown Court tomorrow.

District Judge Steven Hood told him: “The offence of murder can only be heard in the crown court.

“Therefore I am sending this matter to Newcastle Crown Court and you will appear at Newcastle Crown Court tomorrow morning.

“The reason for that is because in matters such as murder, the magistrates’ court cannot consider anything other than a remand in custody, and the law states that a person must appear within 48 hours before a crown court.”



Huntley was attacked with a metal spike at a workshop on February 26 and suffered severe brain trauma.

The child killer was rushed to hospital with serious injuries and placed in an induced coma.

Sources said medics “worked miracles” to keep the fiend Huntley alive after he reportedly had his “head split in two” in the attack.

But The Sun exclusively reported he was taken off life support after brain tests showed he was in a vegetative state.

The monster died nine days after the attack.



Huntley murdered ten-year-old schoolgirls Holly Wells and Jessica Chapman in August 2002 in a case that horrified the nation.

He had abducted the pals and murdered them after luring them both to his home in Soham on August 4, 2002.

During a two-week appeal to find the girls, Huntley gave TV interviews and joined in searches while his then-girlfriend Maxine Carr gave him a false alibi.

The brute, a caretaker at the local Soham Village College, was arrested after the girls’ bodies were discovered 13 days later.

He later admitted he had returned to the site and set fire to their bodies.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

Huntley was convicted in 2003 of the girls’ murders and sentenced to a minimum term of 40 years in jail.

Carr was jailed for perverting the course of justice and released in 2004 with a new identity.

News.title: No new terror trial for Kneecap rapper Liam Og O hAnnaidh as High Court rejects appeal on decision to throw out case
News.content:
KNEECAP rapper Liam Og O hAnnaidh will not face a terror trial after judges at the High Court rejected a Crown Prosecution Service appeal against the decision to throw out the case.

The rapper, who performs under the stage name Mo Chara, was accused of displaying a flag in support of proscribed terror organisation Hezbollah at a gig at the O2 Forum in Kentish Town, north London, on November 21 2024.





But the case was thrown out in September last year, with chief magistrate Paul Goldspring ruling the proceedings were “instituted unlawfully”.

The Crown Prosecution Service appealed against the decision at the High Court at a hearing in January, with the Kneecap rapper opposing the appeal.

In a judgment on Wednesday, two judges at the High Court upheld the decision and dismissed the CPS appeal.

Judge Goldspring had agreed with O hAnnaidh’s lawyers that prosecutors needed to seek the Attorney General’s permission to charge the rapper before informing him on May 21 that he would be charged with a terror offence.



This permission was sought and given the following day, which the court heard meant the charge fell outside the six-month time frame in which criminal charges against a defendant can be brought.

Lord Justice Edis, sitting with Mr Justice Linden, said in Wednesday’s decision that “the judge was right to hold that he had no jurisdiction to try any summary-only offence alleged to have been committed on that date”.

He said: “The respondent has not been tried for his alleged conduct on September 21 2025 and will not be tried.

“He has not been convicted, and he has not been acquitted.”



In a statement provided through his lawyers, O hAnnaidh said after the decision: “I owe eternal gratitude to my legal team, who left no stone unturned in ensuring justice was upheld.

“This entire process was never about me, never about any threat to the public and never about ‘terrorism’, a word used by the British Government to discredit people you oppress both in Ireland and across the world.

“It was always about Palestine and about what happens if you dare to speak up.

“About what happens if you can reach large groups of people and expose their hypocrisy.

“I will not be silent. Kneecap will not be silent.”

News.title: Should Keir scrap 5p fuel duty hike? VOTE NOW
News.content:
POLL OF THE DAY Should Keir scrap 5p fuel duty hike? VOTE NOW

THE Prime Minister has suggested the 5p fuel duty discount could carry on for longer amid worries about the Iran war.

The controversial tax hike is “under review” as the Middle East conflict has triggered pump prices to soar.



An onslaught from both Reform and the Tories in recent weeks has heaped pressure on Ministers as the public struggle with the cost of living.



The 5p fuel duty cut – brought in after Russia’s invasion of Ukraine in 2022 – will gradually come to an end from September under current plans.

But the PM said: “We will keep the situation under review in light of what is happening in Iran.”

The discount will end in September when 1p per litre will be added to pump prices, followed by 2p in December before a final 2p in March next year.

Amid the conflict in the Middle East, unleaded petrol has risen by 3.5p per litre since US-Israeli strikes on Iran started the conflict.



Oil prices hit £89 per barrel but has now eased back to around £67 per barrel.

Surging energy costs will also have knock-on effects on the weekly shop going up in price and holiday prices rising.

Experts said that an extra one per cent could be added to the expected two per cent inflation rate by the end of the year.

Have your say in our exclusive poll.



Love to debate? Join Sun Club for just £1.99 a month for the boldest opinion on the issues shaping Britain.

News.title: Dragons’ Den entrepreneur wins trademark court battle with rival inventor over ‘Piddle Patch’ dog toilet
News.content:
A DRAGONS’ Den entrepreneur has won a court battle with a rival over her “Piddle Patch” dog toilet.

Rebecca Sloan, 39, was handed a £50,000 investment on the BBC show by Stephen Bartlett in 2022 for her product.





She had trademarked the “Piddle Patch” name for the toilet – real turf in a biodegradable litter tray to housetrain dogs – in 2016.

Rebecca ended up in a High Court trademark fight with rival indoor dog toilet-maker, Laurencia Walker-Fooks, and her company City Doggo Ltd.

Through her own company Makeality Ltd, she accused Laurencia of deliberately infringing her trademark in online marketing material in a bid to capitalise on Piddle Patch’s fame.



A judge has now ruled in Rebecca’s favour – meaning Laurencia will now have to pay compensation, which is currently undecided.

District Judge Araba Obodai said she “knew exactly what she was doing” when she began the “campaign of infringement.”

She said using the phrase “piddle patch” in website domain names and embedded in pages was a “deliberate attempt…to benefit commercially from the use of the trademark” by driving traffic to her own company’s site.

The judge added: “It had the desired effect because, as the claimant pleaded, the result was that City Doggo’s website was ranked alongside the claimant’s when consumers searched for Piddle Patch.”



Piddle Patch is a biodegradable litter tray with real grass, which allows pooches to urinate indoors without making a mess while being housetrained.

The court heard Rebecca got the idea for the natural dog toilet while visiting a park.

She secured offers from four dragons when she appeared on the show in 2022 but ultimately took Barlett’s investment.

In response, Laurencia said she came up with her own dog toilet known as “Oui Oui Patch” – after experiencing the “stress and anxiety” of housetraining her dog, Tinkerbell.

The two women ended up going head to head after Rebecca accused Laurencia of infringing on the Piddle Patch trademark.



The words had been used on online marketing materials for the Oui Oui Patch, while domain names using the words had been bought and directed traffic to Laurencia’s site.

This included source code, landing page titles and blog posts, while two of the Piddle Patch domain names were purchased by City Doggo within three days of the Dragons’ Den appearance.

Laurencia admitted using the words but said it was only for search engine optimisation and also so minor that it was “not actionable” in trademark law.

But District Judge Obodai said: “When I listened to [Laurencia] give evidence, I gained the distinct impression that she was seeking to persuade the court that she was naïve in relation to matters of business and intellectual property. I was not persuaded by her portrayal.

“In my opinion, she knew exactly what she was doing when she started City Doggo and started what [Rebecca’s barrister] described as, ‘a campaign of infringement beginning in May 2022’.”



The case will now return to court for an assessment of the compensation Laurencia must pay, although it has already been limited to a maximum of £10,000 by a previous judge.

Speaking after the ruling, Laurencia said: “This was a dispute between two small companies in the Small Claims Track. Judge Hacon said from the beginning there was unlikely to be much at stake in this claim, and it was narrowed further when the claimant dropped its allegation that our product “Oui Oui Patch” copied their trademark.

“The remaining issues in the claim related to errors in our digital marketing. We acknowledged these issues from the beginning but demonstrated their limited visibility. For example, one complained of blog had five views in a month. We corrected these things nearly two years ago.

“I’m delighted Oui Oui Patch lives on and I’m excited as ever to keep helping dog owners toilet train apartment puppies quickly and naturally.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“I am reviewing the judgment and considering appropriate options, including a possible appeal.



“I respect the court’s decision, however I do not recognise its characterisation of my intentions and conduct. Everyone who has worked with me knows that I do business openly and transparently. I have never previously worked in digital marketing and it does not overlap with my professional experience at all.”

News.title: At least 18,000 people evacuated from German city after massive 550lb unexploded British WW2 bomb is found
News.content:
THOUSANDS of people have been evacuated from Dresden after an unexploded World War 2 bomb was uncovered in the German city.

A bomb squad was set to try to defuse the 250Kg British bomb which was unearthed during work in the city centre to rebuild a collapsed bridge.





An exclusion zone was established by 9am local time this morning, cops said.

The fire brigade in Dresden says this is the city’s largest evacuation to date, affecting around 18,000 residents and tourists.

Some of the city’s most famous buildings – the iconic Frauenkirche Lutheran church, the Residenzschloss Palace and the Semper Opera – have been taped off as part of the operation.

More than 400 police, a helicopter and a drone joined the mission to check homes, shops, schools, care homes and offices were empty inside a one kilometre radius.

It is not yet clear how the devise will be defused.

Because the bomb’s detonator is damaged, a water jet cutter has to be used which will “naturally delay” the operation, police spokesman Marko Laske told public broadcaster MDR.

If that doesn’t work, bomb squad experts will have to consider detonating the bomb on site, he added.



Dresden was heavily bombed by the Allies on February 13 and 14, 1945, killing up to 25,000 people and destroying large parts of the old town known for its Baroque architecture.

World War II bombs were previously found and defused at the site in January and August 2025, with thousands of people affected each time.

A bomb was also unearthed in Berlin last year with 20,000 people forced to evacuate the German capital.

Police sealed off the busy Mitte tourist district after divers discovered a massive bomb under four metres of water in the Spree river.

Officials declared residents in the Fischerinsel island neighbourhood were in “mortal danger” and told 7,500 people to leave their homes immediately.



The lockdown shut government offices, embassies, and Berlin’s city hall, while boat traffic on the Spree was suspended.

Subways and streets were closed, and long queues formed at emergency shelters in Mitte town hall and a nearby school.

The discoveries highlight the grim legacy of Berlin’s wartime past.

The city was hit by 363 Allied air raids, and unexploded bombs are still regularly unearthed.

In 2024 alone, 1,600 bombs were defused in North Rhine-Westphalia.



RECOMMENDED STORIES

In June 2025 Cologne evacuated 20,000 people after three wartime bombs were found.

Large parts of Cologne’s city centre, including hospitals, care homes and a hotel, were cordoned off as experts tried to defuse the explosives.

News.title: Map reveals where SNOW will hit UK this week as Met Office issues warning for ‘severe’ 80mph gales
News.content:
FREEZING temperatures are set to bring snow to some parts of the UK this week.

The Met Office has warned Brits to brace for wintry conditions.





A North-Atlantic breeze is expected to hit the UK later this week, bringing with it snow and gale force winds.

According to the Met Office’s forecast for this week, Brits can expect cloudy and windy weather this week, with snow expected to hit Scotland and Northern Ireland.

Harsh conditions can be particularly expected in northern Scotland and across the Outer and Inner Hebrides.

Warnings for “severe gales” of up to 80mph are in place for parts of the UK today.

It is expected that the rain and wind will move southeastwards overnight, with cold temperatures settling in tomorrow.

Friday is forecast to be “rather cold”, but broken up by some sunny spells with the wet weather still persisting.

It is expected that the weekend will bring drier conditions and the rain will clear up.

This is not the only weather warning in place, with flood alerts reaching the South of England over the coming days.



The Met Office said: “Ongoing groundwater flooding is expected for parts of the South of England through the next five days, with ongoing river flooding impacts also possible across parts of Somerset.”

This comes after yesterday’s warning of “wintry hazards” across Britain.

Jason Kelly, Chief Operational Meteorologist for the Met Office said: “A notable shift in wind direction to a north-westerly flow will bring a markedly colder day nationwide on Friday.”

Kelly also noted the potential for a combination of sunny spells, heavy rain, strong gales, and snow over high ground.

News.title: Magaluf 'wolf pack' rape gang's shocking response to questions on British teen attack
News.content:
Magaluf 'wolf pack' rape gang's shocking response to cops on Brit teen attack

The British victim was found sobbing on the floor of the hotel in Magaluf by a security guard after being subjected to the horror attack by the gang of eight men

View 3 Images One of the eight men being brought before the court during the trial (Image: MGE/SOLARPIX.COM)

A vile 'wolf pack' gang who raped a British teenager in Magaluf gave a chilling response when asked about their crimes.

‌



Five of the gang, consisting of seven French men and one Swiss national, were handed sentences between nine and 11 years in prison at the Audiencia Provincial court in Palma, Majorca this week after being convicted of the horrific attack on a young British holidaymaker in August 2023. Another admitted to a sexual assault that didn't involve penetration, receiving a four-year prison sentence.

‌



All eight admitted to filming the sickening gang rape in room 108 of the BH Mallorca Resort, and two of the men posted the footage on Snapchat.

‌



And it has emerged that many of the cowardly attackers refused to co-operate with police during the investigation, or later the courts, attempting to maintain their silence throughout.

View 3 Images Several of the gang of eight men refused to co-operate with investigators, insiders say (Image: MGE/SOLARPIX.COM)

Sickening reality of party town Magaluf from gang rape to 'manosphere' terror READ MORE :

Horror as couple held hostage in their home and forced to hand over £800,000 Bitcoin READ MORE :

One source said: "Some have never agreed to answer questions or give statements.

‌



"These men appear on the surface to be regular young guys who don’t come from broken families and have never tried to allege they were acting out of character by trying to link what they did to the abuse of alcohol or drugs. It's such a shocking case."

French tourists Khalil Abderrahmen Mejbri and Romain Charles Michel Galatioto "orally penetrated" their victim, who had been drinking heavily and during the half-hour-long gang rape "fell into a state of unconscious".

Galatioto did so seven times while the teenager was naked apart from her bra, according to court documents all the men have signed as part of their plea bargain deals.

‌



Frenchman Anthony Guy Gerard Wengler and Swiss national Lucas Haiaiel Ngandjeu Djamen Tchatchou raped her at "least once", as well as Thomas Eric Coretin Wisniewski who is also from France.

View 3 Images Police on patrol in Magaluf (file photo) (Image: AFP via Getty Images)

Tchatchou and Wengler spat at the victim and whipped her, along with Mejbri, after Frenchman Satilmis Sahan slapped her.

‌



A security guard at the hotel found the victim sobbing on the floor in the foyer when he arrived for his shift, and reported the incident to police. Two of the suspects were tracked down abroad on a European Arrest Warrant and extradited, while six were detained in Majorca.

At least six of the men were aged between 18 and 26 when they were arrested, and none of the eight had criminal records in Spain at the time.

Mejbri and Wisniewski were given a nine-year prison sentence for a crime of "sexual assault with carnal access", a severe form of sexual crime involving non-consensual sexual penetration of the victim. Wengler, Tchatchou and Galatioto were handed 11-year jail sentences after admitting the same offence.

Article continues below

Sahan admitted a crime of "sexual assault without penetration” and was sentenced to four years. The eight men convicted also have to compensate their victim with more than €100,000 (£86,000).

News.title: Subway fans say their 'life is a lie' after seeing how baguettes are made
News.content:
Subway fans say their 'life is a lie' after seeing how baguettes are made

Subway fans have been left gobsmacked after discovering how the fast-food chain prepares its baguettes each morning, and it's making some rethink their next sandwich order

View Image People are shocked to learn how Subway makes its baguettes (Image: Getty Images)

Subway fans have been left gobsmacked after seeing how their baguettes are prepared before the store opens to customers. At Subway, customers can customise their own sandwich – from choosing the bread to selecting the fillings and sauces that go into it.

‌



This means diners can enjoy a different sandwich each time they visit while sticking to their personal taste preferences. However, after seeing how the chain prepares its baguettes in the morning, some customers have admitted they are thinking twice about ordering another sandwich. In a TikTok video shared by @soobway_2.0, a Subway worker is seen unboxing frozen baguettes and placing them into a purpose-made mould ready for baking.

‌



The caption reads: "Is that how you thought they were made?"

‌



While some Subway fans said the process is common across fast-food chains, others were shocked, admitting they had assumed the bread was made from scratch each morning.

One user commented: "Wait I thought that was hotdogs."

Another added: "So it's not made fresh!"

‌



A third said: "How come there is nothing actually freshly prepared in restaurants any longer?"

One more person wrote: "My life is a lie."

Content cannot be displayed without consent

‌



Another user joked: "Did people actually think they were kneading the dough in the back?!?"

A final commenter added: "So it comes frozen and they just cook it... Suddenly Subway is no longer worth anything to me. To think they even ask for tips now."

Meanwhile, another worker claimed this technique is no longer used at their store, writing: "We no longer do it like that anymore."

‌



A statement from Subway reads: "Subway serves freshly made sandwiches at a great value to millions of guests around the world every day.

"Restaurants are owned and operated by a network of thousands of dedicated Subway franchisees who are passionate about consistently delivering a high-quality, convenient guest experience and contributing positively to their local communities."

In 2021, the company launched Eat Fresh Refresh, aiming to "improve across every aspect of the business from menu updates to digital and guest experience".

‌



At the end of last year, Subway permanently added jacket potatoes to its UK menu following a "mash-hit" trial.

The decision came after a survey of 2,000 adults revealed Brits will consume more than 8,000 baked potatoes during their lifetime, with a quarter admitting they would eat one every day if possible.

Cheese and beans emerged as the most popular topping (56 per cent), followed by tuna mayonnaise (36 per cent) and bacon (23 per cent).

Article continues below

Cathy Goodwin, interim director of culinary and innovation for Subway EMEA, said: "The nation's love of jacket potatoes is unparalleled.

"The enthusiasm we've seen on social media and the strong demand from our guests throughout the trial made it clear that Spudway deserved a permanent place on our menu."

News.title: Worrying Princesses Beatrice and Eugenie claim emerges as they're 'barred' from events
News.content:
Worrying Princesses Beatrice and Eugenie claim emerges as they're 'barred' from events

Princesses Beatrice and Eugenie face being snubbed from four major royal events including Royal Ascot and Trooping the Colour as their names appear in Epstein investigation documents

View 4 Images Sarah Ferguson took her daughters to lunch with Epstein in Miami (Image: Getty)

The repercussions of Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor's ill-judged friendship with sex offender Jeffrey Epstein have yet to significantly impact his two daughters, even though recent disclosures have now entangled Andrew's ex-wife Sarah Ferguson in the scandal.

While Princesses Beatrice and Eugenie appear to have been less affected rumours suggest they will be excluded from a number of key royal events this year.

However, according to the Daily Telegraph's executive editor Camilla Tominey, that might be about to change. She explained that several royal insiders have hinted behind the scenes that Beatrice and Eugenie may face questions.

Reading out a text message she received from a "royal insider" on the Daily T podcast, Camilla warned: "The York sisters stuff is going to get a lot worse.

"They've escaped a lot of the scrutiny, but there's a lot of information on them and their associations with Epstein along with their parents."

View 4 Images There's concern that there may be more news to come about the Yorks' involvement with Epstein (Image: Getty)

Beatrice and Eugenie are known to have accompanied their mother on a visit to see Epstein in Miami shortly after he was released from prison on a sex charge.

At the time of that visit, Eugenie was 19 and Beatrice was 21. "They weren't five-year-old girls when they were taken to see Epstein. They were grown ups," asserts author Andrew Lownie, whose book Entitled triggered a wave of revelations about Andrew.

There is no evidence of wrongdoing from either sister.

Beatrice and Eugenie were also reportedly enlisted by Epstein to host some of his unnamed associates and provide tours of Buckingham Palace. "There does seem to be this rumbling idea that there is more to discover," says the Telegraph's Royal Editor Hannah Furness.

Hannah emphasised that whilst palace sources suggest the York sisters are "up to their necks" in the Epstein scandal, no evidence has emerged proving they knew exactly what the convicted sex offender was doing across his various properties worldwide.

View 4 Images The scandal of Andrew's association with Epstein has fatally damaged his reputation (Image: Getty)

The "mood music" surrounding the York sisters appears to have shifted in recent weeks, Hannah says. When in October last year, His Majesty the King removed his younger brother's princely status, Beatrice and Eugenie kept their own titles and it was indicated that, for them, it would be "business as usual.

"That seems to have gone a little bit quiet in the last few weeks," Hannah added. "I'm not sure the girls feel as supported as they did by the Palace."

With uncertainty about how much endorsement they currently receive from King Charles, Hannah said: "They're sort of in this vacuum of suggestions and rumours and questions, but not very many answers for them and that must be frustrating."

There's considerable doubt as to whether the sisters will attend Royal Ascot this year and certainly minimal prospect of them participating in the traditional carriage procession with the senior royals. In the most recent set of documents linked to the Epstein investigation, both Beatrice and Eugenie's names crop up frequently - with Eugenie's name reportedly appearing over 300 times.

Whilst the mere mention of someone's name in this vast array of papers doesn't imply guilt, in the world of royalty, which is under constant scrutiny, image is everything.

View 4 Images The sisters are unlikely to be seen with His Majesty at Royal Ascot (Image: Getty)

A royal insider told The i newspaper that whilst Beatrice, 37, and Eugenie, 35, might attend Royal Ascot privately, they're unlikely to partake in the ceremonial proceedings before each day's racing. They stated: "They might attend the races among the crowd but they can't be seen in the royal carriage procession with all this going on."

At present, there's no anticipation that either princess will be in attendance at the Trooping the Colour ceremony in June.

Royal biographer Russell Myers pointed out that although Prince William had previously invited his cousins to help him and his wife Catherine host a garden party at Buckingham Palace, it seems almost certain that this summer he will follow his father's lead. "He can't be seen to be acting out of turn against the King, and there is no indication that he wants to," he commented.

Article continues below

According to the Mail on Sunday, the Prince and Princess of Wales are now determined to keep Beatrice and Eugenie "at arm's length" at least "for the rest of the year". The Sunday Times, meanwhile, reports that the Prince and Princess of Wales had been "careful" to avoid being alongside the princesses as far back as Christmas Day at Sandringham.

As details from the Epstein investigation continue to emerge gradually, the senior royals are likely to maintain their current cautious approach.

News.title: Shocking moment armed cops burst into class after teen girl 'stabbed by boy, 16'
News.content:
Shocking moment armed cops burst into class after teen girl 'stabbed by boy, 16'

Terrifying images show armed officers storming Thorpe St Andrew School, Norwich, as schoolchildren hid under their desks after a violent incident involving a young girl

View 4 Images Armed officers were seen swooping on Thorpe St Andrew School, Norwich, today after a teenage girl was stabbed (Image: X)

Terrified pupils hid under their desks and texted their parents begging to go home as armed officers stormed their school today after a teenage girl was 'stabbed'.

Terrifying images show firearms police storming a school before a 16-year-old boy was arrested just before 10.30am. Schoolchildren were ordered to hide and turn off their phones while teachers barricaded classroom doors when after reports that the girl had been injured.

Officers raced to the scene at Thorpe St Andrew School, Norwich, as around 100 parents gathered after hearing about the incident on social media. One parent was heard shouting: "Just f*****g let the kids out." Another said: "If my son is under a table and someone’s coming with a knife, I want him to be aware they’re coming." One pupil who made contact with their family sent a text reading "I am very scared", while another sent a message saying they "just want to go home".

View 4 Images Panicked parents raced to the school just after 10.30am after learning on social media about the stabbing (Image: Newsquest / SWNS)

Police drones were also on the scene as officers searched for the suspect, who is thought to have fled after jumping over a fence, before an arrest was made. It's understood that the suspect was on the run for more than an hour, with the arrest being made at around 11.30am.

Officers said a 16-year-old boy was being quizzed on suspicion of causing grievous bodily harm with intent. The teenage girl, understood to be a pupil at the school, was taken to the hospital with "minor injuries".

Darren Evans, a father whose daughter is in Year 8, told the Eastern Daily Press: "The communication from the school has been shocking. If it were not for Facebook and the news, I would have had no idea what was going on. I last heard from my daughter an hour ago. There's been a total lack of communication."

View 4 Images A cordon was thrown up around the site and police confirmed a 16-year-old boy had been arrested (Image: Newsquest / SWNS)

Another parent added: "To hear nothing from the school, it’s terrifying. Your mind sort of starts wheeling, you've got kids in the school, you don't know what they want. To have no information whatsoever about what was going on, with all this going on in the world right now, your mind instantly goes to the worst."You automatically boot the worst, and think something horrendous has happened. I mean, it has. You automatically think it's something a hell of a lot worse."

One teacher at the school said they had been told to stay in the classroom with pupils, telling ITV: "One of the things you practice, but never think you'll do." One Parent, Adam Howlett, said that he had still not heard from his daughter, despite his son saying he is still locked in his classroom.

He said: "When it comes to your child even minor injuries are terrifying. You don’t expect to come to school and get injured. I’m just waiting to find out some news and put my mind at rest." Another parent, Shantelle Taylor, said she was "absolutely fuming" to hear about the incident through a friend and not through the school.

View 4 Images Police said the stabbing was "not believed to be linked" to reports of anti-semitic chants at an under-15 football match last week (Image: PA)

The school, which was rated Good in its 2025 Ofsted inspection, is an 11-to-18 mixed secondary school which also includes a sixth form. A Norfolk Police spokesperson said: "A boy has been arrested following an incident in Thorpe St Andrew.

"Police were called to a school on Laundry Lane, Thorpe St Andrew at 10.24am this morning following reports that a teenage girl had been stabbed. Emergency services attended the scene including fire and ambulance. The victim has been taken to hospital with minor injuries.

"A 16-year-old boy has been arrested on suspicion of causing grievous bodily harm with intent. He has been taken to Wymondham Police Investigation Centre for questioning. This incident is not believed to be linked to a hate crime reported at the same school last week."

Article continues below

A statement from Broad Horizons Education Trust, which runs Thorpe St Andrew School, said: "We have clear procedures in place and our staff acted swiftly, with the whole school going into lockdown with children staying in their classroom with their teacher.

"One of our students was injured and is now being treated in hospital for minor injuries. This has obviously been a distressing experience for our whole community and our focus now is on making sure all students feel supported as we begin to understand what happened."

News.title: Dr Amir Khan says your allergy or cold could actually be this 'silent' condition
News.content:
Dr Amir Khan says your allergy or cold could actually be this 'silent' condition

In severe cases this condition could increase your risk of cancer

View 3 Images Dr Amir issued a warning about a lesser-known "silent" condition (Image: Getty)

A doctor has warned that what you think could be an allergy or cold could actually be a “silent” health condition. Doctor Amir Khan shared how to spot silent reflux.

‌



In a video uploaded to social media platform Instagram, Dr Amir explained more about this little-known condition. Dr Amir, who is best known for his appearances on ITV, said: “Are you constantly clearing your throat, have a horse voice, constant, sore throat, phlegm a feeling of something stuck in your throat or a wheeze that just won't settle?

‌



“Well, you might have something called silent reflux, let's get into it.” He continued: “Now, you may have heard of normal acid reflux when acid rises from the stomach into your food pipe or oesophagus, and causes that discomfort or burning sensation in the top of your tummy area or chest - heartburn.

‌



“But silent reflux, also known as laryngopharyngeal reflux, is different.” According to Dr Amir, in this condition, the acid creeps further up, beyond your food pipe.

Content cannot be displayed without consent

Instead of causing heartburn and indigestion, it rises towards your voice box, your throat and your sinus area, irritating “all of them”. Often this condition can be missed or mistaken for something else.

‌



Dr Amir said: “It's called silent reflux because it doesn't give you the typical heartburn symptoms.”

Symptoms of silent reflux

He then listed some of the key signs of silent reflux as:

A hoarse voice

The feeling of something in your throat

A chronic cough or sore throat

Post-nasal drip

Wheeze

Frequent upper respiratory tract infections

‌



He said: “Remember, you might think you have allergies or a constant cold, but it could be this silent reflux. “

View 3 Images Less severe cases of laryngopharyngeal reflux can be treated with medication (Image: Getty)

Causes

There are multiple potential causes of silent reflux. “Now, you get silent reflux because there are problems with the sphincters or muscular valves whose job it is to stop acid from going beyond the stomach”, Dr Amir said.

‌



“You have one here at the bottom of your food pipe or oesophagus. If this one isn't working properly, acid rises up and causes heartburn.

“But you also have a one at the top of your food pipe here, a second one. And if this one isn't working properly, the acid rises higher and causes all of these silent reflux symptoms.

“Now our throat and sinus, and nose area don't have the same levels of protection against acid as our food pipe does, so we get symptoms.”

‌



View 3 Images Drinking a lot of alcohol can cause laryngopharyngeal reflux (Image: Getty)

Things that can cause silent reflux include:

Lots of alcohol

Smoking

Being overweight

Pregnancy

Certain medications like ibuprofen, steroids and antidepressants

Lying down too quickly after a big meal

Hiatus hernia

Drinking lots of coffee

‌



Risks

If left untreated, silent reflux can cause serious problems. Dr Amir said: “There are reasons why silent reflux is a problem.

“The rising acid can interfere with how mucus is cleared from our upper airways and when mucus isn't cleared properly, it can lead to infections.

Article continues below

“The acid can also cause damage to our vocal cords, too, and over time, this chronic inflammation increases the risk of cancer in this area. And finally, the acid might find its way through our windpipe down into our lungs, where it can cause inflammation and lung problems, too.”

If you think you have silent reflux, Dr Amir urged you to see a GP.

News.title: Peter Mandelson demanded £500,000 payout after sacking over Epstein ties
News.content:
Peter Mandelson demanded a payout of more than £500,000 after he was sacked by Keir Starmer as US ambassador over his ties to Jeffrey Epstein.

The first batch of Mandelson files released today show the Government agreed to hand a £75,000 golden goodbye to the Labour grandee. This included £40,330 as three months pay in lieu of notice and a £34,670 termination payment.

However the dossier shows Lord Mandelson tried to get a significantly higher payoff at taxpayers' expense. The files state: "Negotiations began with a request by the individual to pay out the remainder of the 4-year salary costs of the fixed term appointment. This would have amounted to £547,201." Emails show officials discussed mentioning his initial demand to pay out his contract showed they had done "v well to get this settlement down this low with minimal fuss".

Lord Mandelson was initially paid £157,000 a year for the plum post in Washington, which rose to £161,318 in April 2025. The details were revealed in a 147-page dossier published today after MPs compelled the Government to publish files relating to his appointment as US ambassador to Washington.

Image: PA Wire) PA Wire)

The Labour grandee was sacked by the Prime Minister last September after No10 claimed new details had emerged over his friendship with the notorious paedophile Jeffrey Epstein. But Mr Starmer faces serious questions about what he knew when he appointed him.

It comes after Lord Mandelson, 72, was arrested on suspicion of misconduct in public office in February after allegations he leaked sensitive information to Epstein while serving in Gordon Brown’s government.

Some files have been withheld for national security or foreign relations reasons, but the final say over which documents are redacted rests with Parliament's Intelligence and Security Committee.

The Government has also agreed a framework with Scotland Yard on which documents can be released without prejudicing the ongoing police investigation.

Chief Secretary to the Prime Minister, Darren Jones, told MPs the due diligence report on Lord Peter Mandelson "did not expose the depth and extent" of his relationship with Jeffrey Epstein.

Mr Jones told the Commons: "The victims of Epstein have lived with trauma that most of us can barely comprehend. They've had to relive it again and again, and they have had to see accountability delayed and too often denied.

"We must all learn this hard lesson and a culture which dismisses women's experiences far, far too often and too easily, Peter Mandelson should never have been appointed, and the Government will comply with the humble address."

He also said the Government is "exploring whether the committee can tighten rules on lobbying and paid advocacy to bring the Lords in line with the Commons procedures".

News.title: 'I am a psychic and America will take over two key countries this year'
News.content:
'I am a psychic and America will take over two key countries this year'

A psychic has shared her predictions for the United States in 2026, claiming major territorial changes are coming alongside a health crisis that could see JD Vance become temporary president

View 3 Images These two countries could be the next at the hands of Trump (Image: Getty Images)

As US politics grows increasingly unpredictable under President Trump's leadership, 2026 is already emerging as a pivotal year for global affairs – and this psychic believes the upheaval won't end there.

‌



Indeed, her visions for what the coming year holds for the United States forecast substantial transformation, including the possibility of a new temporary president.

‌



The psychic and medium has refined her abilities for over a decade and, utilising her distinctive capacity to perceive the future, has offered a prediction on current events.

‌



Operating as Psychic Kelli online, she delivered numerous forecasts for the nation's trajectory, particularly concerning its disputes with other countries.

View 3 Images Trump unsuccessfully tried to buy Greenland during his first presidency (Image: Anadolu via Getty Images)

@psychickelli on TikTok informed her followers: "I think America will somehow get a hold of Greenland and/or Cuba this year. And I don't think the United States' allies are going to take too well to that."

‌



She added: "And as a result, there's going to be a lot more protesting and national and civil unrest."

Contributing to the discussion about Greenland specifically, Craig Hamilton-Parker, labelled as the 'new Nostradamus', has also made earlier prophecies regarding the territory's future. He outlined how the landmass holds significance for the president in a recent YouTube video.

Octopuses next in line to rule the world, Stone Age construction workers, how to cure a psychopath, and why humans don't have a mating season - all this and more in our latest science newsletter

‌



He said: "My first reaction was that this has little to do with the military, even though Greenland is strategically important. It's really about real estate – oil reserves and, most importantly, rare earth metals. Developing and processing these resources is difficult, which makes new sources extremely valuable."

Though his forecasts point towards some form of negotiation or agreement, rather than a territorial seizure. He shared: "A deal will be struck, potentially involving very large payments connected to Greenland's resources."

View 3 Images Trump unsuccessfully tried to buy Greenland during his first presidency (Image: AP)

‌



His earlier forecasts have carried considerable credibility, given he anticipated another strike on Iran in March 2026. Prior to the recent assaults, he stated: "We will see very targeted attacks – not just on the leadership, but also on the remaining nuclear facilities, because they cannot be left intact."

Meanwhile, Kelli's forecasts were equally dramatic, with predictions indicating the President will suffer a fall, quite literally by tumbling and striking his head. She describes witnessing him falling and requiring hospitalisation, with JD Vance assuming responsibility during his absence.

Indeed, the psychic ventures as far as to forecast when she believes this will occur - identifying August 12 as a crucial date in the diary. According to her, there is a solar eclipse within Leo during this period, which is a moment that triggers unexpected revelations about those in power.

Article continues below

This includes "those who deserve authority and those who can no longer be trusted." She further states: "This is because pride, ego, and image are absolutely going to be tested."

News.title: Weather warnings issued as gales set to batter parts of the UK
News.content:
Gusts up to and in excess of 70mph (113km/h) have been recorded in northern and western Scotland on Wednesday morning as a deep area of low pressure passes to the north of the UK.

The strongest winds are easing, however, and a Met Office yellow warning here expired at midday.

Nevertheless there could still be some minor travel disruption with cancelled ferries, flight delays and bridge restrictions for high-sided vehicles continuing into the afternoon.

While Wednesday will be a dry and bright day for most of the UK, there will be some frequent showers across Scotland.

On Thursday new yellow severe weather warnings have been issued by the Met Office.

The first comes into force at 05:00 GMT across North Wales, northern England, southern and eastern areas of Scotland and will stay valid until 20:00.

Winds will particulary strengthen as an area of intense rain moves south-eastward.

Gusts 50-55mph (65-89km/h) will be seen widely, up to 60mph (97km/h), or even 70mph (113km/h) possible around coasts and hills.

Additionally there is another yellow warning issued for the whole of Northern Ireland valid from 06:00 to 12:00 GMT.

Here there is potential to see a short period when gusts may reach 60mph (97km/h) accompanied by heavy rain. Winds will then ease from the west during the late morning.

Some travel disruption is expected on Thursday due to the wind with bridge restrictions and some delays on the ferry and rail network possible.

Over the weekend another deep area of low pressure will pass to the south of Iceland with strong winds on its southern flank across the UK.

News.content: She told MPs on the Treasury committee earlier on Wednesday that it is "much too early" to guess where petrol prices will be in September and she is "very loath to spend government money on something that the market should be doing".

Boxing.title: Anthony Joshua quits the UK to move to Dubai - even as thousands try to flee amid rising panic in Middle East warzone
Boxing.content:
Anthony Joshua quits the UK to move to Dubai - even as thousands try to flee amid rising panic in Middle East warzone

Boxing superstar Anthony Joshua is quitting the UK to move to Dubai - even though the desert city is now in a warzone with thousands of Britons desperate to get out.

The former heavyweight world champion, 36, has formally confirmed he will now be resident in the United Arab Emirates despite it currently coming under nightly attack from Iranian suicide drones.

Joshua, who was born in Watford of Nigerian heritage, won a gold medal at the 2012 London Olympics and has since been awarded both OBE and MBE honours - but now is quitting his home country.

The surprise move by 'AJ' - as he is known to fans - is revealed in the small print of paperwork he filed on Friday for his £150million business empire in which he is legally required to state the country in which he lives.

And, extraordinarily, the documentation shows that Joshua has pressed ahead with his decision to change his residential status despite the war between the USA and Israel and Iran which has seen neighbouring UAE come under sustained attack.

Anthony Joshua is moving to Dubai - as revealed in the small print of his paperwork

Footage taken from social media shows Saturday's drone strike on Dubai International Airport

As well as the weather and facilities, Dubai is popular with the wealthy because there is no income tax there.

Joshua would have been liable to pay UK tax on his dividend payment of £10.1million in 2024 and £6.3million in 2023.

His company Sparta Promotions also made profits of £20.396million and paid £6.65million in UK tax in 2024.

Joshua confirmed the decision in the same week that recent UK sport emigre Rio Ferdinand and his wife Kate spoke about what it is like to live in Dubai while it is under missile attack.

Those attacks have seen thousands of Britons living in or visiting Dubai when the war broke out left desperate to get out - with some paying as much as £100,000 to secure evacuation on a private jet.

Filings for two of Joshua's companies, Sparta Promotions Limited and 258 Investments Limited, both show the formal change in where he will from now on reside.

Joshua has long been a regular visitor to Dubai where he uses the renowned sporting facilities for holidays and training, as well as promotional work for brands he represents.

The most spectacular stunt in this vein came in 2017 when he undertook a training sparring session on the helipad of the famous sail-shaped Burj Al Arab hotel - a 90-foot wide landing platform suspended 700 feet above the sea.

Passengers in the terminal at Dubai Airport on Saturday after the airport closed again due to drone strikes

He visited the city late last year, dining out with boxing promoter Eddie Hearn, and again last month when he had training sessions with MMA fighter Khabib Nurmagomedov.

The striking decision to shift base to Dubai follows the tragic road accident in Nigeria last year in which claimed the lives of two close friends, personal trainer Kevin 'Latz' Ayodele and long-time therapist Sina Ghami.

Joshua has been in recovery ever since the ordeal and recently posted pictures of rehab work on his ribs via Snapchat.

He captioned the post: 'Rehabilitation protocol. When you go thru certain things, you realise that you are stronger than you think you are.'

The move makes Joshua the latest high-profile wealthy Briton to be based overseas following the likes of Manchester United owner Sir Jim Ratcliffe and EasyJet founder Stelios Haji-Ioannou, who both live in Monaco, and Pimlico Plumbers tycoon Charlie Mullins, who is in Marbella.

The rules on tax for those with ex-pat status are complex.

If individuals spend 183 days or more in the UK they are considered a UK tax resident, but if it is fewer than 16 days they would not be considered resident and eligible for taxes.

For in-between cases, HMRC officials have a complex 'Statutory Residence Test' to determine whether UK tax has to be paid, weighing factors like how much time is spent in each place and how established their ties are in both countries.

Joshua is pictured emerging from the car crash that claimed his friends' lives

Joshua pictured with friends Latif Ayodele (centre) and Sina Ghami, who both died in the crash

So to maximise any tax saving Joshua could be forced to spend barely two weeks a year here.

In December it was revealed that 'AJ' had spent £1.2 million to secure a 15,300 sq ft penthouse, the Sky Palace, in the new Park Lane 101 development in Sultan Haitham City, in Oman, which is 300 miles away.

But it is thought the apartment is an investment property and it is not clear if he has yet settled on a permanent base in Dubai.



There has been speculation that the attacks on Dubai could see property prices there fall by as much as 20%.

Boxing.title: Amir Khan heads to Africa again as boxing promoters battle for control of the sport
Boxing.content:
Amir Khan heads to Africa again as boxing promoters battle for control of the sport

Promoter Amir Khan is staging another boxing card in Africa, positioning himself in a rapidly shifting promotional landscape where global power brokers are increasingly battling for control of the sport.

Khan's latest event, set for Lagos, Nigeria, blends professional bouts with influencer boxing and regional rivalries - highlighting their strategy aimed at tapping into Africa's large youth audience and social media culture.

The card is topped by a crossover bout between Nigerian online personalities Carter Efe and Portable, who together boast around 10million followers and will settle a long-running online feud in the ring. It is being billed as one of the first major influencer boxing contests staged on the continent.

The event arrives at a time when boxing's promotional structure is undergoing significant upheaval. Frank Warren has been involved in a legal dispute connected to the Saudis, while rival promoter Eddie Hearn has also been navigating the growing influence of Dana White's new venture with TKO, Zuffa Boxing.

Against that backdrop, Khan has increasingly looked toward emerging markets rather than competing directly for the biggest heavyweight spectacles in the Middle East or Las Vegas.

His shows in Africa have mixed international fighters with local prospects and internet personalities, aiming to build audiences in regions where professional boxing infrastructure has historically been limited.

Amir Khan's vision for African boxing is moving faster than most realise. Behind the scenes, AK Promotions is already planning to stage four to five shows per year across the continent

October 2025 saw DAZN-broadcast 'Chaos in the Ring' card in Lagos, co-promoted with Dr. Ezekiel Adamu's Balmoral Group Promotions

The likes of Moses Itauma, David Adeleye and Lawrence Okolie all watched the event live

On the sporting side, the Lagos card features several all-African match-ups. Nigerian cruiserweight puncher Ezra Arenyeka (15-2, 12 KOs), known as the 'Nigerian King,' faces compatriot Godday Appah (14-2, 13 KOs) in a bout between two heavy-handed fighters from rival neighbourhoods in southern Nigeria. Both bring knockout ratios above 80 per cent, adding local rivalry to what organisers expect to be one of the night's most intense fights.

Ghana's Elvis Ahorgah (15-4, 13 KOs), who previously fought British contenders Callum Simpson and Tyler Denny in the UK, returns to Africa to face Newcastle's Joe Laws (15-4, 5 KOs) in a super-middleweight contest.

Elsewhere on the card, British southpaw Michael McKinson (27-2, 4 KOs) takes on unbeaten Algerian prospect Mohammad Sahnoun (8-0, 6 KOs), while teenage Nigerian prodigy Raheem Animashaun (19-0, 12 KOs) meets Tanzania's experienced Emmanuel Amos (22-10-1, 13 KOs) in a youth-versus-veteran clash.

Additional bouts include Nigerian prospect Basit Adebayo against Tanzanian veteran Loren Japhet, national middleweight champion Rasheed Adeyemo facing Nicolaus Michael Mdoe, and UK-based Ghanaian welterweight Samuel Antwi meeting Congolese fighter Paul Kamanga.



For Khan, the card reflects an attempt to carve out a niche as boxing's promotional map shifts. While Saudi Arabia has drawn many of the sport's biggest names and purses, emerging markets such as Africa offer promoters a different growth opportunity.

Boxing.title: Inside the shocking twist that has rocked combat sports: Eddie Hearn and Tom Aspinall tell CHARLOTTE DALY why they have joined forces just days after Conor Benn's stunning defection to Zuffa Boxing
Boxing.content:
Inside the shocking twist that has rocked combat sports: Eddie Hearn and Tom Aspinall tell CHARLOTTE DALY why they have joined forces just days after Conor Benn's stunning defection to Zuffa Boxing

Eddie Hearn insists this one isn't about revenge.

But sitting across from the Matchroom boss and the reigning UFC heavyweight champion Tom Aspinall, it is impossible to ignore the wider context.

Matchroom has spent more than four decades building sporting empires, from boxing to darts and snooker, yet the promoter is now embarking on something new.

Matchroom Talent Agency, a fresh subsidiary within the wider Matchroom Sport umbrella, has been created to represent athletes beyond the confines of their sport.

Commercial deals, media opportunities, publishing, digital, broadcast, legal support, fight purse negotiations - the whole ecosystem that surrounds a modern superstar.

And Aspinall, the Salford-born heavyweight king of the UFC, is the first name on the roster - signing a two-year deal with Matchroom Talent Agency.

'It's a two-year deal, a representation agreement that covers every aspect of what Tom does,' Hearn tells Daily Mail Sport. 'On the commercial side, we'll be building his profile, bringing new brands to the table across PR, media, social and digital. But we'll also support the fight side of things.

'This isn't about us stepping in and saying, "Right Tom, we'll go talk to Dana about your career." Andy Aspinall has handled that throughout Tom's career and he'll continue to do so, with our guidance and support. We'll handle the contractual side, our lawyers will review the deals, look at what's next, which fights are on the table, what the purses look like, and make sure everything stacks up.

Tom Aspinall became the first athlete to sign for Matchroom's new talent agency on Thursday

Aspinall hinted at an announcement hours before it was released via an Instagram post

'If Andy is the one having those conversations and we all agree that's the right approach. I'm perfectly happy with that. I don't necessarily need to sit in a room with Dana and negotiate Tom's next fight. But if Tom wants me there, and everyone feels I'm the right person to do it, then of course I'll be there, dealing with Hunter or whoever it needs to be.

'What we're not doing is coming in and saying we're going to break contracts or start arguments with the UFC. Not at all. We're a professional company. We respect what they do and we respect the contract. We'll just have sensible conversations about the value of what is, in my opinion, one of the biggest stars in the sport and more importantly, the flagship division of combat sports: the heavyweight championship.'

Asked whether the new partnership should lead to Aspinall earning significantly more money going forward, Hearn leaves little doubt about his expectations.

'Substantially more. And we’ll show every MMA fighter how we can drive value. I always talk about value. I have looked at some of the contracts he has received to be main event, you'd make three times that for a British title fight.

'Maybe I’m in the wrong business. And by the way, congratulations to them - they’ve made an absolute fortune. But now I’m on the other side, and I represent our guys fiercely to make sure they get what they deserve. When you look at what Tom Aspinall brings to an event and the amount of money that event generates, I don’t think it’s fair. So I have to give him my honest advice from that perspective.'

The venture sits alongside Matchroom's established sporting pillars: Matchroom Boxing, the Professional Darts Corporation, the World Snooker Tour and Matchroom MultiSport.

Many view it as a power move from Hearn who has been back and forth in a verbal war with UFC and Zuffa boss Dana White

Collectively, those properties have delivered some of the most recognisable sporting moments of the past 40 years while turning niche competitions into global entertainment products.

Hearn believes the same thinking can now be applied to individual athletes. Especially Aspinall.

'We’ve been accused by Dana and those guys of lacking vision. I actually think this is a great example of vision. It’s a wonderful move. It’s great for our business, it’s great for Tom Aspinall and, by the way, it’s great for the UFC as well.

'What we’re doing is helping drive the value of one of their fighters. When Tom Aspinall fights again, he’ll be a bigger star than he’s ever been before, which ultimately benefits the UFC. But first we have to make sure he’s getting the respect he deserves from the UFC.

'But not only that. This is a 360-degree management business. We're not just talking about contracts. We're talking sponsorships, brand deals, PR, endorsements, lifestyle, logistics, long-term career strategy.'

It is an ambitious pitch, but one built on Matchroom's long-standing experience of elevating athletes and sports to new commercial heights.

Under the Hearns, darts transformed from smoky pub competitions into sold-out arena spectacles broadcast in primetime television slots. The Professional Darts Corporation distributed more than £19million in prize money in 2024 alone.

Snooker has undergone a similar transformation, expanding aggressively across Asia and the Middle East since Barry Hearn began reshaping the tour calendar into a sponsor-friendly global circuit.

Aspinall (above) has not fought since sustaining a serious eye injury against Ciryl Gane

'This isn't new to us,' Hearn says. 'We've been representing athletes for 40 years in different ways. We know how to build profiles, we know how to deliver commercial strategies and guide careers properly. This is just the next step.'

For Aspinall, it wasn't as straightforward.

'Eddie doesn't actually know this, so this is exclusive, but I went into that meeting in Monaco thinking, I'm not going to do it. Eddie was paying for as a PJ and everything. And I was like I actually feel bad.

'But, I just really liked what Eddie and Matchroom were all about. And I really felt it was right from me, felt right from my dad, felt right for the other team members, and we just all agreed that this is the right move.'

The 31-year-old has become one of the UFC’s biggest names thanks to a string of explosive finishes and his laid-back Mancunian charisma. Yet he has not always felt he has received the recognition he deserves and, after facing criticism following his recent eye injury, Aspinall decided it was time to expand his team.

Still, linking up with Hearn was not a straightforward decision given the long-running feud between the boxing promoter and UFC president Dana White.

'I didn’t want to be part of it, like a pawn in this fight between MMA and boxing, or between Dana and Eddie and all that stuff. I didn’t want to be dragged into that. But Eddie and Matchroom put an offer on the table that I really couldn’t refuse, so I took it.'

Asked whether the partnership with Matchroom could risk souring his relationship with White or affect his standing within the UFC, Aspinall is unequivocal.

Aspinall (pictured on the left) insists he's ready to get back in the cage and 'beat the living daylights' out of UFC rival Gane

'You can choose whatever manager, whatever representation, whatever commercial advisor you want. It's nothing to do with the UFC. This is my business. This is mine and my team's business, and we wanted to expand the team.'

Aspinall goes on to add: 'Fighting is obviously my main focus. That's always number one. But there's a lot that comes with being in this position - media, sponsorships, different opportunities outside the cage.'

Matchroom's global network, he says, offers a platform that could amplify those possibilities.
